summary: Implement Fabric-v2 registry-first cognitive alignment: init taxonomy, structured descriptions, requirement-profile ranking, section-based mandatory injection, and explicit L2 > L1 > L0 precedence.

acceptance_criteria: taxonomy content records L0/L1/L2 origin logic and initial L1 bucket rationale
bootstrap guide includes the three-tier constitution
new nodes can declare domain/module level independent from .fabric/agents path depth
registry mutation validates the new schema

implementation_scope.objective: Schema-ize descriptions and add profile ranking
acceptance_criteria: description supports summary, intent_clues, tech_stack, impact, must_read_if, entities
fab_plan_context returns ranked candidates with match reasons
rule sections such as [MANDATORY_INJECTION] can be parsed
a batch API/tool returns selected sections by stable_id

key_findings: Current schema uses plain activation.description strings.
Current L1/L2 topology is path-depth derived from .fabric/agents.
Current plan-context has description stubs but no Requirement Profile ranking.
Current init does not generate INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md.
Current rule delivery reads full MD content, not selected sections.
decision_context: Use registry-first plus content_ref instead of storing all rule content inside agents.meta.json.
Generate Requirement Profile server-side with optional client hints.
Add section-based injection as a separate delivery layer.
